sound_type: squawks + whistles + clicks -- audible vocalization. Burst-pulse squawks, frequency-modulated whistles, broadband echolocation clicks. The cetacean silence breaks. E=5
ecosystem: Open water, continental shelf, deep channels. Outer coast through Strait of Juan de Fuca. Occasionally enters northern Puget Sound.
season: Year-round in eastern North Pacific. More intermittent in inland waters. Not a deep-Sound resident.

coordinates.description: Pacific White-sided Dolphin in Strait of Juan de Fuca / outer coast. Continental shelf. Occasionally northern Puget Sound.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST DELPHINIDAE -- dolphin family enters. Third cetacean family (after Phocoenidae 186-187). The cetacean silence breaks: audible squawks, whistles, clicks. 9 consecutive marine mammals (180-188) ties Picidae nonet record.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Lagenorhynchus from Greek 'lagenos' (flask) + 'rhynchos' (snout). obliquidens from Latin 'obliquus' (oblique) + 'dens' (tooth) -- slanted teeth. 'Pacific White-sided Dolphin' for its white flank patches.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Lagenorhynchus -- 6 species worldwide. Pacific White-sided Dolphin is the North Pacific representative.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. Third cetacean family in SPS: Phocoenidae (186-187), Delphinidae (188).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fifth marine mammal family: Phocidae (180-181), Otariidae (182-184), Mustelidae (185), Phocoenidae (186-187), Delphinidae (188). 9 consecutive marine mammals.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 9 consecutive (180-188). TIES PICIDAE NONET RECORD.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 2,000-130,000 Hz. Wide range spanning audible and ultrasonic.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 5,000-20,000 Hz (whistles and squawks in audible range).
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Squawks: 2-30 kHz. Whistles: 5-20 kHz. Clicks: 2-130 kHz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Squawks: 0.1-2 sec. Whistles: 0.2-1.5 sec. Clicks: rapid trains.
song_metrics.note_structure: Squawks (burst-pulse, buzzy), whistles (tonal, frequency-modulated), clicks (broadband, echolocation).
song_metrics.note_types: Burst-pulse squawks (social), frequency-modulated whistles (contact/coordination), broadband echolocation clicks.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Complex social chorus with overlapping squawks, whistles, and clicks from multiple individuals in pod.
song_metrics.timbre: Squawks: buzzy, raspy. Whistles: tonal, rising/falling. Clicks: sharp, broadband. AUDIBLE to humans.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Rich, diverse, socially complex. Audible. Dramatic contrast to porpoise silence.
song_metrics.tempo: Variable. Social contexts produce rapid overlapping vocalizations. Echolocation continuous.
song_metrics.context: Open water, continental shelf. Social interaction, feeding, group coordination. Acrobatic displays.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Whistle repertoire varies between populations. Possible dialect differences.
song_metrics.key_property: SQUAWKS + WHISTLES + CLICKS. FIRST DELPHINIDAE. Cetacean silence breaks. Acrobatic. Pods 10-100+. 9 consecutive marine mammals. E=5. v1.49.329.

pelage.overall: Dark gray back with distinctive pale gray 'suspender' stripe running from forehead to tail. White belly. Light gray flank patch. Prominent hooked dorsal fin.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Pale gray 'suspender' stripes on flanks. White belly. Dark back. Hooked dorsal fin. Acrobatic leaping.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Fast, acrobatic. Frequent leaps, belly flops, somersaults. Bow-rides boats. Visible from great distance due to aerial displays.

physical_description.overall: Length: 1.7-2.5 m. Weight: 75-200 kg. Sleek, muscular body. Short beak. Hooked dorsal fin. Conical teeth (dolphin, not porpoise).
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Gray-and-white coloring with pale 'suspender' stripes. Hooked dorsal fin. Acrobatic leaps visible at distance. Conical teeth.
physical_description.field_id: Medium gray-and-white dolphin leaping from water, often in groups. Pale flank stripes. Hooked dorsal fin. Audible squawks and whistles. Frequently bow-rides boats.

nodes.title: First Delphinidae -- Dolphins Enter
nodes.description: First dolphin in the engine. Third cetacean family. Whistles distinguish dolphins from porpoises. The cetacean silence breaks: audible squawks, whistles, clicks.

nodes.title: Silence to Song -- Cetacean Acoustic Arc
nodes.description: Dall's (186, silent voice + rooster tail), Harbor (187, E=1 silence), Dolphin (188, audible squawks/whistles). Three degrees from silence to voice. The cetacean silence breaks.

nodes.title: Acrobatic Displays -- Leaps and Somersaults
nodes.description: Spectacular aerial displays. Multiple dolphins leaping simultaneously. Cryptic surfacing (187) to full aerial display (188). Visual progression mirrors acoustic.

nodes.title: 9 Consecutive Marine Mammals -- Ties Picidae Nonet
nodes.description: Degrees 180-188. 9 entries. Ties the Picidae nonet record. 5 families: Phocidae, Otariidae, Mustelidae, Phocoenidae, Delphinidae. E=1 to E=6.

cross_references.relationship: CETACEAN PROGRESSION! Porpoise (silent voice, rooster tail E=5) to dolphin (audible voice, aerial display E=5). Same energy, opposite strategy.
SILENCE TO SONG! E=1 (absolute silence) to E=5 (squawks + whistles). The cetacean acoustic arc completes in one degree.
MARINE MAMMAL ERA! 9 consecutive (180-188). First marine mammal to first dolphin. Ties Picidae nonet. 5 families.
THE BREAKTHROUGH! DoNormaal breaks E=7 ceiling (E=8). Dolphin breaks cetacean silence. Both break through. Total E=13 (record).
